decision_target: signal-only versus signal plus a public cancel operation
source_of_truth: Saved generic Lite scalar, stream, nested-close, error, and caller-control probe plus current Lite source
read_method: Run the saved replay checker and compare its JSON result with cancellation-probe.json
questions_answered: Current parent close cleans parent-owned resources before a live scalar child settles or closes.
Current parent close also leaves active streams and three-level nested descendants running after parent resource cleanup.
Current scalar errors, stream completion, producer errors, and consumer break already propagate and clean their own invocation correctly.
A parent signal plus an optional caller signal can cancel one invocation without cancelling its sibling or parent.
Structured close can abort cooperative scalar descendants, terminate active streams, join nested descendants, and then clean parent resources.
The explicit context owner already calls close, the invocation caller can abort its supplied signal, and running flows only need to observe ctx.signal; a public cancel operation adds no caller.
Signal-driven scalar exits need core cancellation classification because the read-only prototype closes them as errors while current stream abort already closes as aborted.
No start, spawn, worker pool, session API, or task handle is required.
questions_unanswered: A scalar promise that ignores its signal cannot be forced to stop; structured close must wait for it, while adapters own any bounded timeout policy.
The exact internal descendant-settlement bookkeeping needs implementation review but must not become a public task registry.
Independent replay has not accepted this checkpoint or its wall reads.
decision: candidate only: add ExecutionContext.signal, add optional signal to exec and execStream options, and make close abort descendants, return active stream iterators, join descendants, classify signal exits as aborted, then clean parent resources; do not add ExecutionContext.cancel, start, spawn, or task handles

wall_gate.reason: Independent replay and integrity verification are pending

risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The current descendant leak count is three in the nested-close replay and parent resources clean before those descendants.
The candidate prototype reduces post-close descendant leaks to zero for cooperative scalar work, nested work, and active streams.
A public cancel method would duplicate context close for owners and would not expose controller-created child contexts to invocation callers.
Optional caller signals preserve selective cancellation without cancelling sibling work or the parent context.
The implementation must preserve existing stream completion, producer-error, consumer-break, and scalar-error behavior.
